title: artemisia maritima subsp. salina
intro: Artemisia maritima subsp. salina, also known as Sea Wormwood, is a subspecies of the wormwood plant that is native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sea Wormwood include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments such as arthritis, indigestion, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Sea Wormwood is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in supplements and remedies.
